welcome!</strong></h4><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\"><strong>Always Live Music</strong> featuring regional musicians.</p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\"><em>Want to try out your moves you\u2019ve learned in class? <br>Come out & dance in an open and supportive environment.<br>Get support on site from Swing It Seattle Managers.</em></p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\">Tickets available for <a href=\"https://www.swingitseattle.com/register/events\" target=\"_blank\">advanced purchase</a> or at the door.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><em>Ticketing Options - Swing It Seattle is committed to making our programming financially accessible while still compensating our musicians fairly for their time & talent.  Please select the pricing level that best fits your budget.* </em></p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"\"><strong><em>https://www.swingitseattle.com/register</em></strong><em><br></em></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Swing It Seattle offers the best and most accessible <a href=\"https://www.swingitseattle.com/swing-dance-history\" target=\"_blank\">swing dance</a> instruction in Seattle. We specialize in the original swing dance, lindy hop. With our loyal and dedicated student base, we have built a welcoming community for all. Swing It Seattle also organizes weekly and monthly live music social dance events at the historic South Park Hall. We are Seattle\u2019s Home for Happy Feet! welcome!</strong></h4><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\"><strong>Always Live Music</strong> featuring regional musicians.</p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\"><em>Want to try out your moves you\u2019ve learned in class? <br>Come out & dance in an open and supportive environment.<br>Get support on site from Swing It Seattle Managers.</em></p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"sqsrte-large\">Tickets available for <a href=\"https://www.swingitseattle.com/register/events\" target=\"_blank\">advanced purchase</a> or at the door.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><em>Ticketing Options - Swing It Seattle is committed to making our programming financially accessible while still compensating our musicians fairly for their time & talent.  Please select the pricing level that best fits your budget.* </em></p><p style=\"text-align:center;white-space:pre-wrap;\" class=\"\"><strong><em>https://www.swingitseattle.com/register</em></strong><em><br></em></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Swing It Seattle offers the best and most accessible <a href=\"https://www.swingitseattle.com/swing-dance-history\" target=\"_blank\">swing dance</a> instruction in Seattle. We specialize in the original swing dance, lindy hop. With our loyal and dedicated student base, we have built a welcoming community for all. Swing It Seattle also organizes weekly and monthly live music social dance events at the historic South Park Hall. We are Seattle\u2019s Home for Happy Feet! As a celebration grounded in SHTEAM (Science, History, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math) we are inviting creatives of all ages to share their work and join the fun. Expanding on last year's spotlight exploring circular economy, creative reuse, and the built environment, this year's theme, \"Break the System\" invites system change. This year we\u2019ve added more categories creating more opportunities for you to participate. To learn more, please explore the Science Fair drop-down menu in the navigation bar to find your niche and to access everything you need to get started: project guides, registration forms, sponsorship options, and more. data-block-type=\"1337\" data-definition-name=\"website.components.html\" data-sqsp-block=\"text\" data-website-component-id=\"156f4358f8f255c177bf\" id=\"block-156f4358f8f255c177bf\"><div class=\"sqs-block-content\"><div class=\"sqs-text-block-container\">\n  \n  <div class=\"sqs-html-content\" data-sqsp-text-block-content><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong>OVERVIEW</strong></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Seattle Center Fest\u00E1l presents Sea Mar Fiestas Patrias in partnership with Sea Mar Community Health Centers.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Sea Mar Fiestas Patrias commemorates the independence of Latin American countries, many of which celebrate their national independence day in the month of September. It is a festival to celebrate history, while taking pride in the new generations of Latinos who now call the United States home.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Throughout the two-day event at Seattle Center, attendees can enjoy delicious traditional Latin American food and cooking demonstrations, live music, traditional folk dance performances, art exhibitions representing Latino culture, free health screenings, children\u2019s activities and more.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong>HISTORY</strong></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Sea Mar began hosting Sea Mar Fiestas Patrias over twenty years ago in the South Park neighborhood of Seattle. Sea Mar extended the celebration to Seattle Center when they joined Fest\u00E1l in 2010. Since then, the festival has been put on every year during one weekend in mid-September. On Saturday, Sea Mar hosts a parade and community festival in South Park, which has become a great tradition that many families look forward to year after year. On Saturday and Sunday, Sea Mar hosts a large festival at Seattle Center with a variety of fun activities for everyone of all ages to enjoy in celebration of Latino culture.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">The festival is an important celebration for Sea Mar because its organization was founded by a group of Latino community leaders in 1978. This group sought to address the healthcare needs in western Washington, specifically among the Spanish-speaking communities. Sea Mar understands the barriers Latinos and other immigrant communities may face when navigating the healthcare system, pursuing a higher education, or simply trying to make a living. For this reason, Sea Mar goes beyond primary care and has established additional programs, services, and events like Sea Mar Fiestas Patrias to celebrate Latino cultural traditions.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong>DID YOU KNOW?</strong></p><ul data-rte-list=\"default\"><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Spanish is the second most spoken language (by native speakers) in the world.</p></li><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">The United States has the second largest Spanish-speaking population in the world after Mexico.</p></li><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Latinos represent the largest minority in Washington state with more than 11% of the total population.</p></li><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Sea Mar Community Health Centers has grown to be one of the largest community health centers in the country, with over 100 locations across western Washington providing medical, dental, behavioral health, housing, educational and cultural services.</p></li></ul></div>\n\n\n  \n<style id=\"container-styles\">[data-website-component-id=\"156f4358f8f255c177bf\"] {\n As a celebration grounded in SHTEAM (Science, History, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math) we are inviting creatives of all ages to share their work and join the fun. Expanding on last year's spotlight exploring circular economy, creative reuse, and the built environment, this year's theme, \"Break the System\" invites system change. This year we\u2019ve added more categories creating more opportunities for you to participate. To learn more, please explore the Science Fair drop-down menu in the navigation bar to find your niche and to access everything you need to get started: project guides, registration forms, sponsorship options, and more. class=\"sqs-block-content\"><div class=\"sqs-text-block-container\">\n  \n  <div class=\"sqs-html-content\" data-sqsp-text-block-content><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">July 7th \u2013 July 12</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">For Students Entering Grades 6-12 in Fall 2025</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">10AM-4PM (Monday-Saturday).</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Participants build their own pinhole cameras, learn how to evaluate lighting and exposure time, explore ideas of visual composition, i.e., what makes a \u201Cgood photo,\u201D and learn how to use the chemistry of a black & white photographic darkroom on site to develop their own photographs.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">In contrast to remote-learning, online simulations, digital, and virtual experiences, this will be a hands-on analog experience.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">The Georgetown Steam Plant visually inspires students with its photogenic, industrial-age architecture and design which truly represent the original \u201CSteam-Punk,\u201D all in support of education, art, and science learning. Students will explore the Georgetown Steam Plant as a scientific, technologic, historic, and creative space.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Instructors: Eric Muhs & Dan Pickard are \u201Cmostly-retired\u201D Seattle Public</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Schools science teachers with nearly 50 years of science teaching and pinhole photography instruction between them. Cost and further details at the website link below. Them</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Additional programs, dates, prices, and to learn more use the link below.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Sign up here: There\u2019s even a Side Show with a reality-show style art contest, aerial acrobats, a flash tattoo station and even more kids and adults art activities and an afterparty at Jules Maes Saloon! data-block-type=\"1337\" data-definition-name=\"website.components.html\" data-sqsp-block=\"text\" data-website-component-id=\"eceec9449a43127ac350\" id=\"block-eceec9449a43127ac350\"><div class=\"sqs-block-content\"><div class=\"sqs-text-block-container\">\n  \n  <div class=\"sqs-html-content\" data-sqsp-text-block-content><h1 style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><a href=\"https://www.riveraccesspaddleprogram.org/events/river-clean-up-ac6ph\">April River clean up!</a></h1><ul data-rte-list=\"default\"><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Saturday, April 5, 2025</p></li><li><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">11:00\u202FAM 1:00\u202FPM</p></li></ul><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">The River Access Paddle Program (RAPP!) is working towards a long-term community goal to promote the Duwamish River as a safe and accessible place to get exercise, build community connections, and share information about the history and environment of Seattle\u2019s only river. We are working with Duwamish River communities to create more access and awareness opportunities on The Duwamish River and the Salish Sea. Through a 2025 Duwamish River Opportunity Fund grant we are able to offer free programs to the public, encouraging health and education around the Duwamish River. </p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">We'll be kayaking to areas only accessible from the water to remove garbage from the shoreline. We'll be visiting the areas around the He?apus Village site, Kellogg Island, and ecological restoration sites in the area. To date we have removed hundred of pounds of trash from the river with the help of our amazing volunteers like you. We\u2019ll provide the kayaks, personal flotation devices and cleaning supplies free for who RSVP. This event is completely free to attend. If you have your own kayak and PFD you may join us on the river. Please still sign the waiver. All are welcomed. Time on the water will be about 90 minutes-2.5 hours. </p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><a href=\"https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdHe2BkW26gdINzzEGq_8HFxKh2cSOya2Auv85FMc7elCDabA/viewform?usp=dialog\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Please use this form to RSVP</strong></a><strong>. Participants must be at least 12 years old and be accompanied by an adult. </strong>You will be contacted 72 hours before the event with directions of where to meet, park and what to bring. </p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">No experience necessary.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">First come first serve. We only have kayaks for 40 people (2 per kayak). Once an event fills up, you may be added to the waitlist.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong>This project is funded in part by the Duwamish River Opportunity Fund (DROF) award from the City of Seattle.</strong></p></div>\n\n\n  \n<style id=\"container-styles\">[data-website-component-id=\"eceec9449a43127ac350\"] {\n    \n    --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px;\n data-sqsp-text-block-content><p class=\"sqsrte-large\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">3/21/25 \u2013 4/6/25  <strong>Global Menus, Local Venues</strong></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">This year\u2019s Plate of Nations will be featuring over 70 restaurants! And this year <span class=\"sqsrte-text-highlight\" data-text-attribute-id=\"cc2f49b3-1e41-48e1-957f-643ea02d6ad2\">includes the South Park neighborhood</span>.</p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">Plate of Nations, the signature event of the MLK Business Association, was designed to <strong>collectively promote independently-owned restaurants </strong>in the MLK community. Immigrants from around the world have settled in the Rainier Valley and started businesses that provide cultural favorites for ethnic communities looking for a taste of home. Those restaurant owners are excited to welcome other customers to join in their traditions.Taste the flavors of South Seattle, support locally-owned small businesses, and share with friends!</p><p style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong><em>The Buildup was created during COVID and was a private event. This show was created for Artists by Artists to express themselves freely and get paid fairly while doing so. This show is a whole community experience. We rotate Artists and give opportunity to our local Chefs to create beautiful locally grown and sourced food for our community members and friends to partake. At this event you will experience live music performed by 3 different artists, small bites prepared by Global Chill, live painting and much more. </em></strong></p><p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\"><strong><em>The Buildup is now being supported by Cultivate South Park.</em></strong></p></div>\n\n\n  \n<style id=\"container-styles\">[data-website-component-id=\"4170d69650f45d35087e\"] {\n    \n    \n    \n\n\n\n  }\n\n  [data-website-component-id=\"4170d69650f45d35087e\"] .sqs-html-content {\n    \n    --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%;\n  --tweak-text-block-padding: initial;\n}\n\n  #block-4170d69650f45d35087e {\n      mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend\n  );\n      border-radius: var(--tweak-text-block-radius);\n  }\n\n  \n  \n#block-4170d69650f45d35087e {\n      --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px;\n    }\n\n\n  \n  \n\n  #block-4170d69650f45d35087e {\n    }\n\n</style>\n\n<style id=\"override-container-styles\">\n    @media screen and (max-width: 767px) {\n          \n            \n\n[data-website-component-id=\"4170d69650f45d35087e\"] {\n            \n            }\n        }\n    \n  \n    @media screen and (max-width: 767px) {\n          [data-website-component-id=\"4170d69650f45d35087e\"] .sqs-html-content {\n            \n            }\n        }\n    \n  @media screen and (max-width: 767px) {\n          \n          \n\n          \n          \n          \n        }</style>\n\n<style class=\"transform-overrides-vars\">\n      \n    </style>\n\n  \n\n\n</div>\n</div></div></div></div></div>","excerpt":"<p class=\"\" style=\"white-space:pre-wrap;\">The Buildup was created during COVID and was a private event.
